preamble: Epenthesis of [f] is a rare transitional process, occasionally found in English dialects or historical Germanic developments as a cluster-simplification strategy.

examples.note: In 'sixth': [θ] → [f] via TH-fronting in Cockney/Estuary English. Note: this is a substitution (θ→f), not true [f] epenthesis (∅→f); flagged as mislabeled.
